untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of earnings announcements and surprise magnitude relative to consensus forecasts. These are external event data and earnings consensus metrics that are not available in the provided indicator set. The signal also requires intrabar momentum alignment which cannot be precisely quantified with available indicators. Approximated entry using absolute returns as proxy for surprise magnitude (>=1.5% threshold approximates 1.5 std dev moves in typical regimes), but this is a weak substitute for actual earnings surprise data.
translation_notes: This signal fundamentally requires external data sources (earnings dates, consensus estimates, actual reported earnings) which are not part of a standard technical indicator library. The translation uses log_returns as a proxy for unexpected price movement magnitude, but this does not capture true earnings surprise vs consensus. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT as signal specifies price moves in direction of surprise (could be long or short). Exit set to 1% reversal as specified. Hold period set to 10-15 bars max. A proper implementation would require integration with earnings calendar and consensus estimate databases.
